versions.description: ESP-Hosted-MCU provide drivers to act any ESP chipset as Wi-Fi or Bluetooth co-processor.

examples.name: host_bluedroid_ble_compatibility_test
examples.description: This example tests Bluetooth compatibility with mobile phones using ESP32 as a co-processor and ESP32-P4 as the BT Host.

examples.name: host_bluedroid_bt_hid_mouse_device
examples.description: This example shows how to implement a Bluetooth HID mouse device using Classic Bluetooth HID profile APIs.

examples.name: host_bluedroid_host_only
examples.description: This example demonstrates a Bluetooth Host using ESP-Hosted as HCI IO to the BT Controller for ESP32-P4 Dev Board.

examples.name: host_bt_controller_mac_addr
examples.description: This example shows how to change the MAC Address for a BLE device using the ESP32. It can be configured to use either Bluedroid or NimBLE.

examples.name: host_gpio_expander
examples.description: This example demonstrates how to use the ESP-Hosted solution to control the GPIOs of the slave co-processor from the host MCU.

examples.name: host_hosted_cp_meminfo
examples.description: This example enables a memory monitor on the ESP co-processor to track heap usage, allowing queries and periodic reports.

examples.name: host_hosted_events
examples.description: This example uses ESP-Hosted events to detect and manage co-processor resets or hangs, ensuring connectivity.

examples.name: host_manage_copro_ext_coex
examples.description: This example demonstrates configuring and using External Coexistence (EXT_COEX) in the ESP-Hosted co-processor, allowing remote control of the ESP device's Packet Traffic Arbitrator for managing radio access.

examples.name: host_network_split__power_save
examples.description: This example demonstrates how to implement Network Split and Host Deep Sleep in ESP-Hosted systems, allowing power-efficient operation.

examples.name: host_nimble_bleprph_host_only_uart_hci
examples.description: This example demonstrates a BLE peripheral using the UART transport with custom configurations for ESP32 series MCUs.

examples.name: host_nimble_bleprph_host_only_vhci
examples.description: This example uses Bluetooth Hosted HCI transport provided by ESP-Hosted with specific configuration steps.

examples.name: host_openthread_border_router
examples.description: This example demonstrates an OpenThread border router on an ESP32-P4, communicating with an OpenThread RCP and Wi-Fi on an ESP32-C6 co-processor.

examples.name: host_openthread_cli
examples.description: This example demonstrates an OpenThread CLI with additional features, enabling communication between ESP32-P4 and ESP32-C6.

examples.name: host_peer_data_transfer
examples.description: This example demonstrates how to send and receive raw binary data between a host device and a coprocessor, allowing maximum control over data formats.

examples.name: host_performs_slave_ota
examples.description: This example demonstrates ESP-Hosted Transport OTA, updating slave firmware through the existing ESP-Hosted connection without additional hardware.

examples.name: host_sdcard_with_hosted
examples.description: This example shows how to use an SD card on an ESP32-P4 with ESP-Hosted using SDIO for communication.

examples.name: host_shuts_down_slave_to_power_save
examples.description: This example demonstrates host-side control of an ESP slave to reduce power consumption by shutting it down when idle and waking it up on demand.

examples.name: host_transport_config
examples.description: Demonstrates how to configure ESP-Hosted transport interfaces (SDIO, SPI, UART) before initialization.

examples.name: host_wifi_easy_connect_dpp_enrollee
examples.description: This example demonstrates configuring ESP devices as an enrollee using Device Provisioning Protocol (DPP) for secure onboarding.

examples.name: host_wifi_itwt
examples.description: This example shows how to use itwt of wifi, working only in station mode with APs supporting itwt.

examples.name: slave
examples.description: The ESP-Hosted MCU Slave Example demonstrates how to provide Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity to host MCUs through ESP32 co-processors using SDIO, SPI, or UART interfaces.
